questions.description: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded same-sex couples in relation to the 'shared home' in Ireland.
questions.text: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded same-sex couples in relation to the 'shared home' in Ireland. Support your answer with reference to appropriate authority.

questions.description: In 2000, Janet decided to start growing some organic vegetables in a field behind her house.
questions.text: In 2000, Janet decided to start growing some organic vegetables in a field behind her house. After she had them all planted, she had a small shed erected to store her gardening tools in. A couple of months later, Janet planted a large hedge along the boundary to act as a windbreak. John, the registered owner of the field, lived in Spain. While holidaying in Ireland, John drove past the field only to see all the work that Janet had done on it. Janet happened to be out pulling up some weeds at the time. John told Janet that he had no present use for the field so she could continue her great work but he did intend to seek planning permission to build a house on it in the future. In the meantime, John told Janet that he would be happy to grant her a short lease. Janet asked him to write to her solicitor about this but despite the fact that negotiations followed, no lease was ever granted. Janet died in 2012, leaving all her property to her daughter, Sheila, who had resided with her. John died in 2008, leaving all his property to his son Dermot. Dermot is in poor health and lives in a nursing home. Sheila seeks your advice on whether or not she can be registered as owner of the field.

questions.description: Critically examine current Irish Landlord and Tenant Law having regard to the following: (a) The influence that Deasy's…
questions.text: Critically examine current Irish Landlord and Tenant Law having regard to the following: (a) The influence that Deasy's Act 1860 has had in the past and continues to have on the question of landlord and tenant law in Ireland. [And]{.underline} (b) The role that the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 currently plays and will play in the future.

questions.description: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Tom and Jerry were joint tenants of a… Advise the respective executors as to who…
questions.text: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Tom and Jerry were joint tenants of a house in the Cork suburbs. Last year, they were travelling on an airplane which crashed. Both were killed instantly when the plane on which they were both travelling struck a mountain. In his will, Jerry left all his property to his friend, Charles. By his will, Tom left all his property to his two siblings George and Fred. Advise the respective executors as to who is entitled to the house. [And]{.underline} (b) Catherine died unexpectedly from a short illness in February 2013. At the time of her death, she was living on the family farm (which she had inherited some 30 years previous) with her husband Tommy. In her will, Catherine left all her worldly belongings to the cats and dogs home. Catherine has a daughter, Julie, from a previous relationship to whom she left nothing under the will. Upon discovering her mother's death, Julie claims she was left out of the will as she did not get along with her mother. However, she does acknowledge that her mother gave her €15,000 over 10 years ago for college expenses and living costs. Advise Julie.

questions.description: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Critically analyse the rules and principles which govern the construction of wills…
questions.text: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Critically analyse the rules and principles which govern the construction of wills under Irish Succession Law. [And]{.underline} (b) Briefly discuss the rules and principles which dictate how an intestate's estate should be distributed.

questions.description: One bright spring morning, Matthew went out for a ten mile jog on his land. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to…
questions.text: One bright spring morning, Matthew went out for a ten mile jog on his land. Feeling particularly energetic on this particular morning, he continued on into the next field running over a sign which read "Keep Out - No Trespassing". Matthew paid little attention to this but soon after, he tripped and fell over something jutting out of the ground. Upon closer inspection, he realised that it was an antique gold pendant which had an engraving on the back "To my true love T'. Bob, the owner of the field in question, was out checking on his cattle and upon hearing the noise, ran over to Matthew to check if he was ok. He noticed the pendant in his hand covered in mud and claimed that it belonged to his great great grandfather - Trevor. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to the pendant.

questions.description: Critically evaluate the creation and effect of rights of way and the right to light and the right to support, making…
questions.text: Critically evaluate the creation and effect of rights of way and the right to light and the right to support, making possible suggestions for reform.

questions.description: Jack successfully applied for a mortgage in 2006 so that he could build an… Mortgage.
questions.text: Jack successfully applied for a mortgage in 2006 so that he could build an extension onto his shop premises in a small village in Co. Sligo. Over the past few years, Jack used up all of his savings to cover his mortgage repayments in the absence of any profit being generated from his business. Unfortunately over the past year, he has started to fall into arrears on his mortgage repayments. Advise the bank what its options are in terms of enforcing its security on the loan to Jack. Refer to legal and policy developments in your answer.

questions.description: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Julie died a widow last month.
questions.text: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Julie died a widow last month. By her will, she left all of her property to her only son, John. However, John died last year, survived by his wife Jacinta and his son, Mike. By his will, John left all of his property to Jacinta. Discuss. [And]{.underline} (b) One morning, following a news report of yet another natural disaster causing numerous deaths, Jenny decided that she had better draft her will before something happened to her. She had no paper so she wrote out her will on the back of a box of Weetabix. Instead of her signature which she didn't like the look of, she marked the end of the will with an "X". Later that morning, her two closest friends called over, Tom and Jerry. Jenny pointed out to them where she had signed the will and asked them to sign as witnesses. Jerry, who was celebrating his 18th birthday that week, received a mobile phone call from the bakery, after which he left in a hurry before he could sign the will. Jenny was anxious to get her will finalised and so called over to Jerry's house that evening to obtain his signature. Jerry signed the will as requested. A couple of days later, Fred, a neighbour, told Jenny that her will was not valid as it was written in pencil. Jenny replied "You might as well tear it up and throw it away then". Fred crumpled the will up in a ball and left it on the table. A couple of days later Jenny was killed in a tragic car accident. Discuss the validity of Jenny's will.

questions.description: Describe and discuss the methods by which a mortgagee may enforce his or her security.
questions.text: Describe and discuss the methods by which a mortgagee may enforce his or her security.

questions.description: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) In 1990, Jack and Jill purchased a 100 acre farm as tenants in common in equal…
questions.text: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) In 1990, Jack and Jill purchased a 100 acre farm as tenants in common in equal shares in County Sligo. For a number of years, they lived happily on the farm together but then in December 1995, Jill left after a major row and emigrated to New Zealand. Jill has recently returned to Ireland and now seeks your advice as to what to do in relation to the farm. In particular, she would like to know if she can force a sale or require Jack to pay a rent to her. She would like to know if there are any other options open to her. [And]{.underline} (b) Discuss two ways by which co-ownership may be brought to an end.

questions.description: Critically analyse the Irish statutory provisions governing land registration, with particular reference to security of…
questions.text: Critically analyse the Irish statutory provisions governing land registration, with particular reference to security of title and the transfer of property in Ireland.

questions.description: James owned a small apartment in an old complex in Dublin city centre. Advise Claire as to what rights, if any, she has…
questions.text: James owned a small apartment in an old complex in Dublin city centre. Fed up with the Irish weather, James went to live in Canada in 1998, making no arrangements for the upkeep of his apartment. In January 2002, Paddy, a student, noticed the' apartment was unoccupied for some time and decided to move in. He redecorated it and continued to live in the apartment until he finished his degree in May 2004. Claire, a friend of Paddy's, moved into the apartment the day after he left. In December 2013, Claire received a letter from James' solicitor requiring her to vacate the apartment as soon as possible. Claire ignored the letter and continued to occupy the apartment. At the end of January 2014, Claire received a second letter asking her to vacate the premises as soon as possible. Advise Claire as to what rights, if any, she has over the apartment.

questions.description: Easements are usually defined according to the presence of four key characteristics.
questions.text: Easements are usually defined according to the presence of four key characteristics. In your opinion, does this categorisation provide a useful or appropriate analysis of easements? Discuss with reference to relevant legal authority.

questions.description: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Describe and discuss the rights of a surviving spouse or civil partner to…
questions.text: Answer (a) [and]{.underline} (b) (a) Describe and discuss the rights of a surviving spouse or civil partner to appropriate the family home or shared home under section 56 of the Succession Act 1965. [And]{.underline} (b) Assess the impact section 117 of the Succession Act 1965 has had on curtailing a testator's testamentary freedom. Support your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: Dominic owns a large farm in Co. Advise Hans.
questions.text: Dominic owns a large farm in Co. Longford. In 2006, he purchased the property next door to his farm which contained an old dilapidated cottage. Dominic used the old cottage to store farm tools and hay bales. During the summer months, he permitted passing holiday-makers to camp in the grounds of the cottage. Hans, one of the campers, decided to explore the cottage one summer afternoon. As he entered the house, he stumbled and got his foot caught inside a rotten floorboard. As he was trying to release his foot, he noticed something shiny at his foot. He discovered a gun (which later turned out to be an old antique) and a large sum of money. He brought the discovery to the attention of the Gardaí, who then proceeded to question Dominic. Dominic, believing that there may be some illegality attached to the gun, denied any knowledge of the find. In the absence of any further evidence, the Gardaí retained the gun for further investigation, but returned the money to Hans. Upon discovering this, Dominic now argues that the money belongs to him. Advise Hans.

questions.description: According to the Property Registration Authority, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive and well…
questions.text: According to the Property Registration Authority, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive and well developed system of land registration. Since the foundation of the Land Registry in 1892, there has been a gradual, ongoing and continuous programme of movement away from the older and limited system of recording Deeds (in the Registry of Deeds), to the more modern, flexible and comprehensive 'title registration; system provided through the Land Registry. In light of the above statement, describe and discuss the system for recording property transactions in Ireland.

questions.description: In December 2010, Rachel had to go into hospital to have a brain tumour… Advise John and Mandy.
questions.text: In December 2010, Rachel had to go into hospital to have a brain tumour removed. She decided that given the risks associated with the procedure that she should make a will. Rachel was very nervous in the days leading up to the procedure so the doctor gave her a light sedative. In the meantime, Rachel's good friend, Peter who was a solicitor, came by the hospital to see her and to talk to her about drawing up the will. Since Rachel was acting a bit drowsy, Peter offered to sign the bottom of the will for her. With Rachel's consent, Peter did so with Rachel's name, while at the same time, signing his own name. He then asked the doctor and nurse who were tending to the patient in the bed next to Rachel's to also sign the document. Neither the doctor or the nurse looked at the document - they just signed it as they were engaged in deep conversation with their patient. Rachel left everything she had to her husband John (worth approximately €500,000). She made no provision for: her daughter, Mandy, since she had supported her for years while in boarding school. In August 2012, Rachel discovered that John was having an affair with his secretary and threw him out of the house. She phoned Peter, her solicitor, and asked him to destroy the will she made in 2010 and to write a new one, leaving her entire estate to the cats and dogs home. Following the phone call, Peter tore up the will and left it in a bag for shredding. He then drew up the new will as requested by Rachel. However, that evening, Rachel was knocked down and killed while walking the dog. She had not signed the new will. John and Mandy both claim that they should be entitled to something from Rachel's estate. Advise John and Mandy.

questions.description: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant measures for dealing with the private rented sector…
questions.text: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant measures for dealing with the private rented sector in Ireland, in particular, in the area of security of tenure and tenancy termination procedures. Consider whether the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 has succeeded in its aim of providing a clear legislative framework in respect of tenancy obligations for landlords and tenants in Ireland.

questions.description: In 2000, Joe, a farmer, sold one of his fields to a large commercial… Company.
questions.text: In 2000, Joe, a farmer, sold one of his fields to a large commercial development company - RJ Developments Ltd. The field was adjacent to a main road which the local authority had indicated would be upgraded to motorway status at some stage in the future. RJ Developments Ltd purchased the property from Joe with the intention of building a large motorway service station on the site. Once the sale of the land was complete, RJ Developments Ltd e-mailed Joe asking him if he could remove his cattle from the field and requesting him to place one of their advertising signs on the gate nearest the road. However, Joe never checked his e-mails since he did not 'do that technological stuff'. In fact, due to various delays in the motorway development, Joe continued to graze his cattle on the land for a number of years'. In 2006, Joe decided that a change was needed and he left farming. He sold his cattle and went on a round the world trip. Before he left, he made an agreement to lease the field to his best friend and neighbour, Frank, so that he could grow some mushrooms. Frank put a number of mushroom tunnels in the field to serve as growing rooms. In 2009, the Chief Executive of RJ Developments Ltd was driving by the field and noticed that there was some kind of funny looking structure in it. He parked the car and looked in over the gate at the structure in the field. He decided to notify his solicitor who wrote to Frank requesting that he leave this private property immediately. Frank replied and said he had intended on leaving anyway as he was getting tired of the mushroom farming and in any case, more traffic on a motorway would ruin his crops. At the beginning of this year, RJ Developments Ltd has finally started on the motorway development but Frank has decided he wants to remain on the land to continue to grow organic vegetables, which have been flourishing in the field. Advise RJ Developments Ltd.

questions.description: Outline and assess the Irish Courts' approach to the admissibility of extrinsic evidence in the construction of wills.
questions.text: Outline and assess the Irish Courts' approach to the admissibility of extrinsic evidence in the construction of wills.

questions.description: 65-year-old Lucy has been employed as a live-in housekeeper for Mat for the past 30 years.
questions.text: 65-year-old Lucy has been employed as a live-in housekeeper for Mat for the past 30 years. When she first started working for Mat, Lucy received £20 per week. This was never increased. Over the years, Mat became increasingly dependent on Lucy for things that went above and beyond the role of housekeeper. In fact, Lucy had become Mat's full-time carer rather than his housekeeper. Despite the fact that there was no conversation to this effect, Lucy claims that Mat had led her to believe that upon his death, Lucy would be entitled to the house or at the very least, she would have an unequivocal right to remain in it. Lucy stated that this was a mutual understanding between the parties. Mat has now died and has left all of his property to a local charity. Lucy seeks your advice on whether she has a claim to any interest in the house.

questions.description: Should the contractual licence be regarded as an interest in land in Irish Law?
questions.text: Should the contractual licence be regarded as an interest in land in Irish Law? Discuss your answer with reference to applicable case law.

questions.description: In 1980, Jenny built a small house on a site, much of which was bogland,… Advise Jenny.
questions.text: In 1980, Jenny built a small house on a site, much of which was bogland, adjacent to Fred's land. The conservatory of the house had a large bay window that overlooked Fred's land which was a large field with some really beautiful flowers and a stream at the end of it. The only access route to the nearby road is through Fred's field, because the driveway which Jenny built on her own land is almost always flooded during the year. Jenny has therefore continually used the field since 1982. This year, Fred has sold the field to a developer whose plans for development include the erection of a 12 foot high boundary wall. The wall will cut off the field access to the road and it will considerably reduce the amount of light received through the bay window and destroy Jenny's beautiful view. Advise Jenny.
